rules.recommendation: Explain FL Studio CPU as real-time buffer processing pressure, not total OS CPU usage.
rules.rationale: The CPU Meter transcript and manual distinguish FL Studio's audio deadline from OS-wide CPU utilization.
safety_limits: Read-only explanation until a stable CPU metric API is available.

rules.recommendation: Treat clicks, pops, stuttering, and underrun counts as live playback issues; rendered glitches should be investigated as plugin behavior.
rules.rationale: The performance manual states underruns do not occur in rendered audio because rendering can take as long as needed.
safety_limits: Do not mutate project state while diagnosing without a rollback-safe fix plan.

rules.recommendation: For manual guidance, use Intel/AMD buffers at least around 10 ms and Apple Silicon sample choices of 128, 192, 256, 512, or 1024 depending on project load.
rules.rationale: The official optimization and audio-settings pages distinguish Intel/AMD and Apple Silicon buffer behavior.

rules.recommendation: Prefer 44.1 kHz or 48 kHz for typical production performance; avoid 96/192 kHz unless there is a specific need.
rules.rationale: The official optimization manual warns that high sample rates require substantially more calculation.

rules.recommendation: Suggest Smart Disable as manual CPU guidance only with plugin-specific caution.
rules.rationale: The manual says Smart Disable can reduce CPU but may interfere with time-based, envelope-based, or long-decay plugins.
safety_limits: Warn about plugins cutting out, timing issues, or long tails.

rules.recommendation: For heavy CPU plugins, prefer independent mixer-track paths where musically appropriate and avoid unnecessary shared dependencies.
rules.rationale: The optimization manual says each mixer track can be an opportunity for parallel processing, while linked tracks and shared sends can serialize work.
safety_limits: Do not reroute for CPU alone without user approval and rollback-safe grouped writes.
Musical routing intent takes priority over speculative CPU optimization.

rules.recommendation: Flag many Audio Clips using real-time Stretch or Stretch Pro modes as a possible CPU issue, but keep changes manual until API support is verified.
rules.rationale: The optimization manual warns Stretch and Stretch Pro consume far more CPU than Resample when not needed for tempo changes.
safety_limits: Do not claim automatic Stretch/Stretch Pro read or write support.
Follow existing audio-clip API limitation policy.
